publishedAbstract: * Storage Pools with Error: `...failed to reconcile CephFilesystem... Error EINVAL: device class hdd does not exist.` * The `defaultCephDeviceClass: hdd` value is immutable, preventing ssd from updating in the CephFS, CephBlockPool, and RGW storagepool CRs. **Related Articles:** [Override device rotational flag in OCS/ODF environments](https://access.redhat.com/articles/6547891) [Ceph- Device class is displaying wrong hardware class for an OSD](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/3341491) [ODF: Changes in crush rules due to device class changes result in PGs being in an unknown, misplaced, and/or remapped state](https://access.redhat.com/solutions/7098573) [Supported configurations for Red Hat OpenShift Data Foundation 4.X](https://access.redhat.com/articles/5001441)
publishedTitle: The "defaultCephDeviceClass: hdd" in the StorageCluster CR Prevents Reconciliation of Ceph Storage Pools "Error EINVAL: device class hdd does not exist" - OpenShift Data Foundation (ODF)
